verdict_reasoning: GYPA is a classical single-pass transmembrane sialoglycoprotein of the erythrocyte membrane with a heavily O-glycosylated extracellular N-terminal domain bearing MN and Ss blood group antigens. CD235a designation and HGNC gene-group membership in 'Blood group antigens' and 'CD molecules' confirm cell-surface localization. Blood group antigen function — recognized by alloimmune antibodies and determining transfusion compatibility — is only possible if the protein is stably surface-accessible via its own transmembrane anchor.
